verifications.evidence: TASK-001 (skeleton-config bootstrap, landed at ab05a0e): npm run build + npm run check + npm test all green (68 pi-context tests, 0 fail). Runtime-demoed via the pi-context-cli on throwaway substrates: custom-vocabulary path (context-init -> amend-config add block_kind, no accept-all) reached state 'ready'; init -> accept-all preserved the minted substrate_id (sub-776038675a8a52ee) with idempotent re-run (adopted:false) and a single registry entry; layers-only and status_buckets-only configs classified non-skeleton. A fresh adversarial re-audit confirmed the isSkeletonConfig registry-coverage fix complete (all 13 registries, derived from REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS) with no new regression.

TASK-002 (switch identity reconciliation, landed at 17d9a3b): npm run build + check + test green. Runtime-demoed via the pi-context-cli on throwaway substrates for all three switch paths: (1) switch onto a config-bearing-but-unregistered substrate -> substrate_id registered, context-validate clean; (2) wrong-dir registry entry -> context-validate still reports substrate_id_registry_mismatch (reconcile does not overwrite); (3) switch - (to_previous) onto a pruned/unregistered previous substrate -> reconcile re-registers it, validate clean. An adversarial probe first found switchToPrevious unwired (false-drift reachable via switch -); after wiring it, a fresh adversarial re-audit confirmed all six attack vectors hold, all three switch paths self-heal consistently, the no-previous error path still throws without reaching reconcile, and best-effort guard skips unloadable/invalid configs without breaking the switch.

TASK-006 (op-contract DispatchContext / cli-arc alpha, landed f5b6bd5): npm build/check/test green across packages (incl. new ops-registry.test.ts ctx-builder branches, cli attestation, step-block workflow stamping). Runtime-demoed via the built cli: append-block-item with NO created_by/created_at onto an attested-required schema now SUCCEEDS and stamps created_by=human/<explicit-or-resolved> (the pre-alpha AJV failure); buildDispatchContextFromExecute resolves human (auth-gate params.writer) / agent (ctx.model.id) / pi-agent fallback; malformed --writer errors instead of stamping human/undefined. A fresh adversarial probe verified all 10 write ops stamp with ctx in the correct arg slot, --dryRun preserved on amend-config + write-schema, read ops + promote-item/write-schema-migration + context-switch + context-archive unregressed, and created_by preserved on update; two non-functional findings (stale comment, malformed-writer rough edge) were fixed and re-verified.

TASK-007 (cli-arc beta, op-surface coverage, landed 56b153d): npm build/check/test green. Runtime-demoed via the built cli on throwaway substrates: remove-relation surgical (only the exact edge), replace-relation atomic re-orient, append-relations bulk+dedup, upsert-block-item append->update with created_by preserved + modified_by updated (attested); full step-by-step custom-vocabulary substrate creation entirely via the cli (init -> write-schema -> amend-config -> write-block -> append-block-item). The probe->fix->re-audit loop caught and fixed: the foldin string-endpoint writer (now structured), an oid-gated proxy that wrongly dropped resolvable-but-unbackfilled edges (now authoritative substrateHasItem on all three detection paths), and a write-gate that didn't persist a drop-only change. A clean final adversarial re-audit found nothing new across the write-gate scenarios, the 3-path class closure, and the beta ops. OP_COVERAGE_RULE + the 7-entry allowlist establish the FGAP-009 contract gamma will enforce.

TASK-008 / FGAP-010 (gamma op-coverage parity check, landed dc7f514): scripts/parity-check.ts AST-enumerates pi-context library writers and classifies each against OP_COVERAGE_RULE; a silent ctx drop is fatal, a dual-surface optional-param mismatch is reported non-fatally. Verified: npm run build + check + test all green (0 fail across the 4 packages); the standalone run exits 0 on the real tree (70 writers classified: 42 op-backed-transitive, 24 op-backed-direct, 3 intentionally-unexposed, 1 internal-primitive; 0 ctx-drops; 5 non-fatal dryRun divergences surfaced on the relation/upsert ops, tracked as FGAP-012). Catch behavior runtime-demoed: an injected uncovered writer and an injected ctx-drop each fail with exit 1 in throwaway copies. Two fresh adversarial probes: the first caught a structurally-dead divergence detector (op-side run.params.includes was always empty, yielding false-positives including amend-config/write-schema), rewritten to op-schema-property + kebab-flag-literal signals; the re-audit of that fix found no active false-positive or false-negative and confirmed the fatal guards behaviorally unchanged. Wired into husky pre-commit + CI.

TASK-009 (parity-check detector hardening, landed 2298186): opDeclaresParam now resolves object/allOf/$ref via flattenSchemaProperties; scriptParsesFlag rewritten regex->AST (matches the flag only as an ===/== comparison operand or case-clause label, ignoring comments and display strings). Verified: npm run check clean; full suite green (829 + 114, 0 fail); the prior-audit residual repro scriptParsesFlag with a commented === flag now returns false while the real a === flag returns true; parity-check exits 0 unchanged (70 writers, 0 ctx-drops, the same 5 non-fatal dryRun divergences). Two fresh adversarial passes: the first caught the regex residual false-positive (comment/display-string match), which drove the AST rewrite; the re-audit of the AST matcher found no false-negative against the real parse lines (including the value-bearing && form), no residual false-positive (comments, display strings, console.log args, !== all excluded), the fatal guards still firing, and deterministic output. Detection-robustness only; no real-tree change.

TASK-012 (FGAP-013) + TASK-013 (FGAP-015), the complete --json contract, landed 75f2611. Verified: build/check/test green (full suite, 0 fail) incl. new contract tests for the --json value shape, the in-pi Pi-tool over-cap surface, and the parity-check gate. Runtime demo on a 120KB over-cap seed: resolve-item-by-id --json -> 126 bytes (data:null, truncated:true, payload absent, was the 120KB leak); under-cap -> full value; read on both CLI --json and the in-pi Pi-tool path bounded; default REFUSAL/PARTIAL text byte-identical. The gate catches an injected { json: resolveItemById(...) } op (exit 1) and reports 0 {json}-content-cap bypasses on the real tree. Two fresh adversarial re-audits: the first found the {json}/Pi-tool cap-bypass (root cause behind the recurrence), which drove the boundary-cap fix; the second confirmed the recurrence ended — no read/query op ships unbounded data on either surface, the gate+runtime-backstop layered defense holds, {read} unregressed, deterministic, 130 targeted tests pass. Pre-existing truncateHead pretty-vs-compact / line-cap over-refusal nuance tracked as FGAP-016 (non-leak).

TASK-014 (FGAP-014, landed d0ab83d): complete-task migrated to the verification_verifies_item edge-gate. build/check/test green (full suite, 0 fail); runtime demo on a throwaway .context copy (complete-task completes with edge+passed, throws edge-absent without); fresh adversarial audit found no bypass (no-edge/reversed-edge/wrong-relation-type/non-passed/missing/already-done all throw), zero residual target/target_type reads, ctx attested, validateContext invariant complements the gate, 135 tests pass.

TASK-010 (relations dry-run parity, landed 8fcf02d): the 4 relation byRef fns + ops gain a shared dryRun preview; the 4 scripts delegate to it. build/check/test green; runtime demo (append/remove --dryRun preview, relations.json byte-unchanged; real calls still write); parity-check shows the 4 relation dryRun divergences gone (only upsert-block-item remains). Fresh adversarial audit: no dryRun path writes across 4 ops x both surfaces, preview matches real outcome exactly, validation + stdout parity, deterministic.

TASK-011 (upsert dry-run parity, landed 4b72b86): the last dry-run divergence. upsertItemInBlock + op gain a shared dryRun preview; the script delegates. build/check/test green; runtime demo (upsert --dryRun previews, block file byte-unchanged; real writes); parity-check now at 0 divergences (all 5 cleared). Fresh adversarial audit: no dryRun write, validation parity exact, lock correct, stdout preserved, deterministic.

verifications.evidence: TASK-035 (FEAT-006 T2, base-stamping) verified. Commit fa4e7fd wires the dormant content-addressed object store into both baseline-write sites in index.ts: installContext baseline loop (putObject(destRoot, content_hash, schemaJson), reusing the already-parsed body + already-computed content_hash) and the updateContext baseline-refresh (TASK-034 site, inside the !dryRun guard, keyed by the new content_hash). adoptConception is correctly not a site; checkStatus and the installed_from.assets shape (context.ts:79) are untouched (purely additive, Option B from R-0008). Real-substrate tests (install-subcommand.test.ts, tsx --test against source): 'base-stamps each installed schema body under its baseline content_hash' (install round-trip: getObject by the baseline content_hash returns a body whose computeContentHash equals it) and '(b2) a resync base-stamps the resynced schema body under its NEW refreshed baseline content_hash' both pass; the dry-run test extended to assert objects/ gains no entry under updateContext({dryRun:true}). build+check+test green (pi-context 36, fail 0). Fresh-context adversarial probe: PASS, no defects (own harness round-tripped 2 assets; dryRun stamped nothing by independent before/after snapshot; checkStatus byte-identical by construction; install-site put inside the per-schema try/catch; idempotent + content-addressed, no collision; asset shape unchanged). The merge consuming this base is TASK-036.

verifications.evidence: TASK-036 (FEAT-006 T3, deterministic 3-way schema merge) verified. Commit 39d0458: new schema-merge.ts mergeSchema(base, ours, theirs) -> {merged, conflicts} (pure; canonicalJson equality; MISSING unique-symbol sentinel) implements the recursive 3-way: all-three-objects per-key over the sorted union (MISSING-merged keys omitted = deletion); SET-ARRAY (last segment required/enum/type, all sides arrays) = (ours∩theirs)∪(ours\base)∪(theirs\base) sorted; add/remove (one side MISSING) present-on-one->take, both-add-differing->conflict, removed-vs-unchanged->honor, delete-vs-modify->conflict; atomic 3-way for scalars/non-set-arrays/kind-mismatch (all-differ -> {path,base,ours,theirs} conflict). Wired into updateContext's locally-modified/both-diverged arm: base via getObject(content_hash) (null -> refused fallback), ours=installed, theirs=catalog; conflict-free -> writeSchemaCheckedForDir(meta-validate + nested-id guard + atomic write; dryRun validates-only) + result.merged; conflicts -> result.conflicts (no write); throw -> refused; brought_current extended with result.merged so an auto-merged body is re-base-stamped as the next base. UpdateResult gains merged/conflicts; the update op unchanged. Tests: schema-merge.test.ts 15/15; install-subcommand merge cases (d) disjoint auto-merge both survive, (e) same-node conflict bytes-unchanged, (f) enum widening set-union, (g) delete-vs-modify conflict, (h) merged re-baselined+stamped — all pass on real substrates. build+check+test green (pi-context 1066 pass, fail 0). Fresh adversarial probe: PASS, no defects (own merge harness 6/6 + real-updateContext harness 13/13; set-array gated on all-arrays; null vs MISSING distinguished; allOf -> atomic; dryRun/conflict write nothing; changed tests strengthened not weakened). Documented conservatism (not a defect): when the base lacks a set field entirely and both sides add it with different arrays, it is recorded as a conflict rather than set-unioned (safe-conservative; surfaces the divergence). The conflict resolver consuming result.conflicts is TASK-037.

criteria_results.criterion: mergeSchema returns {merged, conflicts[]}: disjoint key additions merge; required/enum/type-union arrays merge as sets; overlapping edits to the same node emit a typed {path,base,ours,theirs} conflict
criteria_results.evidence: schema-merge.ts + 15 unit tests + independent harness 6/6

criteria_results.evidence: case (e): conflict recorded with path, dest bytes unchanged; updateContext branches off checkStatus per asset

verifications.evidence: TASK-037 (FEAT-006 T4, conflict resolver) verified, including the iterate-to-zero fix. Commit 5136685 adds the resolver: a CLI-layer post-pass (cli.ts) over the update op's UpdateResult.conflicts that keeps updateContext pure; pi-context exports getConflictMergeInputs/refreshBaselineForSchema/renderConflicts; pi-context-cli resolveConflicts dispatches an interactive pi-bound mergetool per conflicting schema (read-schema+write-schema are static SKILL-union tools — no --grant; the agent writes via write-schema's existing dryRun + auth-gate; no auth-gate/pi-bound internal change) or renders a {path,base,ours,theirs} report when non-interactive (stdin&stdout TTY && !--json); never hard-blocks (the clean schemas updated in updateContext). Commit e4995cc FIXES a resolved/unresolved detection defect (found by the adversarial probe): the finalize compared on-disk to the recorded baseline, but every schema reaching the resolver is both-diverged (ours!=base before the session) so a no-op session falsely reported resolved and re-stamped OURS as baseline, swallowing the conflict; the resolver now snapshots the installed schema's on-disk hash before/after the session (new export installedSchemaOnDiskHash) and resolves + re-stamps only on an actual change, else unresolved with no refresh (the conflict persists). Tests: resolve-conflicts.test.ts (no-op spawn -> unresolved + baseline unchanged; writing spawn -> resolved + baseline re-stamped to the reconciled body; non-interactive -> report + no spawn) + conflict-resolver-helpers.test.ts; build+check+test green across all packages (fail 0). Two fresh adversarial probes PASS (the resolver, then the fix — own harnesses proving no-op->unresolved+baseline-unchanged and writing->resolved+re-stamped). The real interactive agent reconciliation (a live pi mergetool session + operator-confirmed write) is the credentialed/TTY piece — demonstrated structurally via the writing-fake-spawn (the resolver's dispatch + before/after detection + finalize), not a live pi session in this non-interactive harness.

criteria_results.criterion: on a non-empty conflict set, update dispatches a bounded pi-bound session carrying read-schema + write-schema, feeding (base, ours, theirs); the agent's write passes through write-schema dryRun + auth-gate (non-interactive refuses) with no auth-gate/pi-bound internal change
criteria_results.evidence: resolveConflicts dispatches runPiBound(['-p',prompt]) per schema with base/ours/theirs embedded; read/write-schema are static SKILL-union tools; auth-gate + pi-bound byte-unchanged (probe confirmed)

criteria_results.criterion: with no interactive context, update falls back to a rendered conflict report {path,base,ours,theirs}, leaves the schema unmodified, proceeds with the rest of the update (never hard-blocks)
criteria_results.evidence: non-interactive -> renderConflicts to stdout, no spawn, no writes; the resolver is a post-pass over already-updated clean schemas

criteria_results.criterion: runtime demo: interactive -> agent proposal previewed via --dry-run and applied on confirm; non-interactive -> a clear conflict report + schema unchanged + remaining schemas still updated
criteria_results.evidence: non-interactive report + writing/no-op dispatch demonstrated on real substrates via fake spawn; the live pi-agent reconcile + operator-confirmed write is the credentialed/TTY piece, structurally verified via the before/after detection + finalize

verifications.evidence: FEAT-005's outstanding [orchestrator] real-session smoke confirmed by the operator (davidryan@gmail.com): running pi-context pi-bound from a cwd outside the repo with the published packages present launches an interactive pi session constrained to the composed extension tool surface (the two-package-root SKILL.md tool union + the active substrate's config.tool_operations[] composites), in which the agent can invoke the union tools and cannot invoke tools outside it, with --grant extending the union and passthrough args reaching pi unchanged. The operator ran and confirmed the live interactive/credentialed session — the piece the orchestrator could not exercise in a non-interactive harness; TASK-030 (VER-019) had stub-verified the dispatch/composition path. Recorded at the operator's direction ('done'); the orchestrator did not itself run the interactive session.

verifications.evidence: TASK-038 (FEAT-006 T5, config-registry propagation) verified. Commit 6f9c0cb: new pure mergeCatalogRegistries(existing, catalog) in context.ts drives each registry's identity field from REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS (relation_types/block_kinds by canonical_id, invariants/lenses by id), deep-clones existing, appends only catalog entries whose id is absent, never replaces/removes/body-merges a present entry (additive-only). Wired into updateContext after the baseline-refresh loop with a FRESH loadConfig (so the merge write does not clobber the refresh's installed_from updates), a !dryRun write guard, and a try/catch that preserves the schema-update result. UpdateResult gains registryAdditions; the update op is unchanged (returns the whole result). Real-CLI runtime demo on a copy of the live .context (32 custom relation_types): pi-context update --dryRun reported the one catalog-absent relation_type (item_derived_from_item) + wrote nothing; pi-context update added it (32->33), the custom item_governed_by_convention survived, registryAdditions listed it. Tests: merge-catalog-registries.test.ts 5/5 (additive; present-id-untouched-on-divergent-body; canonical_id vs id; empty/missing; input not mutated) + install-subcommand registry cases (absent reappear; user-added survives; user-modified-divergent-body preserved; dryRun no-write). build+check+test green (pi-context 1083 pass). Fresh adversarial probe PASS, no defects (own harness: divergent-body present entry byte-identical, input not mutated; canonical_id/id keying; the race-safety test confirms both the baseline refresh and the registry add coexist in one update; no-write-when-nothing-absent holds by mtime). block_kind schema/starter materialization for a newly-declared kind is out of scope (filed as a follow-up gap).

criteria_results.criterion: update additively merges catalog-new config-registry entries (relation_types / invariants / block_kinds / lenses) absent from the existing config; it never removes or overwrites a user-present entry
criteria_results.evidence: mergeCatalogRegistries (pure, additive, driven by REGISTRY_DESCRIPTORS) + 5 unit cases + adversarial harness: present id never replaced even on divergent body

criteria_results.criterion: an existing substrate gains the new catalog vocabulary on update; user-added/-modified registry entries are untouched; --dry-run lists the additions and writes nothing (closes FGAP-060)

criteria_results.criterion: build + check + full test pass; runtime demo (existing substrate + a catalog carrying new entries -> entries appear, customizations intact)
criteria_results.evidence: build/check/test fail 0; real-CLI demo + the install-subcommand integration cases on real substrates

verifications.evidence: TASK-039 (FEAT-006 T6) verified — two disjoint sub-fixes in index.ts (commit c31f9a7). FGAP-050 (updateContext / pi-context update): resyncSchema's return widened to {status, registeredMigrations: {schema,from,to}[]}; the version-bump registration loop reports the decls it appends; the same-version arm and the blocked-rollback arm report [] (post-rollback truth — migrations.json reverted). updateContext captures the live registrations onto a new UpdateResult.migrationsRegistered and, on --dryRun, computes the WOULD-register set READ-ONLY via computeWouldRegisterMigrations (catalog chain minus the decls already present, no write). FGAP-051 (installContext / install --update): a computeFileContentHash equality guard before the empty-block overwrite skips rewriting a block already equal to the catalog starter (-> skipped, not updated). The two are disjoint (updateContext never writes blocks; installContext's preserved/installed arms untouched). Real-substrate tests: live (a 1.0.0->1.0.1 version bump reports {schema:tasks,from:1.0.0,to:1.0.1} + persists it to migrations.json), dryRun (would-register reported + migrations.json byte-unchanged), same-version (-> []), blocked at 0.9.0 (-> [] + migrations.json reverted), block-skip (starter-equal empty block skipped, mtime+bytes unchanged; starter-different still updated). build+check+test green (pi-context 1089 pass). Fresh adversarial probe PASS, no defects (own harness: dryRun read-only + dryRun-set==live-set; blocked reverts + reports []; the block-skip is JCS-canonical — a whitespace-different-but-content-equal starter is still skipped; the CLI surfaces migrationsRegistered; all UpdateResult mutation buckets present). A non-blocking biome useOptionalChain warning the test introduced was cleared (split assert).

criteria_results.criterion: update output enumerates every schema re-sync/merge AND every migration declaration registered into migrations.json; --dry-run lists them without writing (closes FGAP-050)

verifications.evidence: FEAT-006 (the unified, drift-aware, customization-preserving pi-context update command with --dry-run) is complete — all six slices done and verified: T1 shell + drift routing (TASK-034/VER-020), T2 base-stamping (TASK-035/VER-021), T3 3-way schema merge (TASK-036/VER-022), T4 conflict resolver (TASK-037/VER-023), T5 config-registry propagation (TASK-038/VER-025), T6 surfaced mutation reporting + idempotent block skip (TASK-039/VER-026). The unified `pi-context update` (a reflected op on the globally-installed CLI) consults the per-schema checkStatus drift classification and: resyncs in-sync/catalog-ahead (migration-aware); 3-way merges locally-modified/both-diverged schemas preserving non-conflicting edits, routing irreconcilable conflicts to a pi-bound agent mergetool or a read-only report; additively propagates catalog-new config-registry entries (relation_types/invariants/block_kinds/lenses) preserving user entries; enumerates every mutation it performs (schema re-syncs/merges via the resynced/migrated/blocked/merged buckets, migration-declaration registrations via migrationsRegistered, config additions via registryAdditions); and --dryRun previews the entire change set while writing nothing. A locally-edited schema, the user's config registries, and populated blocks are never silently clobbered. Build/check/full-test green throughout; each slice carried a runtime demo + a fresh-context adversarial probe (all PASS). FGAP-046/049/050/051/060 closed across the arc.

criteria_results.criterion: pi-context update --dry-run reports the full set of schema/block/migration changes it would make (per the checkStatus drift classification) and writes nothing
criteria_results.evidence: --dryRun computes + reports resynced/merged/conflicts + registryAdditions + migrationsRegistered (would-register read-only) and writes nothing; verified byte-unchanged across T1/T5/T6 dryRun tests

criteria_results.criterion: pi-context update preserves or merges locally-modified schemas (per FGAP-046's chosen route) rather than overwriting them; config registries, populated blocks, and user-added elements are untouched
criteria_results.evidence: T3 3-way merge + T4 resolver (FGAP-046 closed); T5 additive registry propagation never overwrites a user entry (FGAP-060 closed); T6 idempotent block skip (FGAP-051 closed); populated blocks preserved

criteria_results.criterion: Every mutation the command performs — schema re-syncs, migration-declaration registrations, block starters — is enumerated in its output
criteria_results.evidence: resynced/migrated/blocked/merged + migrationsRegistered (FGAP-050 closed) + registryAdditions; block starters via the install-path InstallResult buckets
